points.text: Tonix Pharmaceuticals published Phase 1 clinical data for TNX-1500, an Fc-modified anti-CD40L (CD154) monoclonal antibody, demonstrating a favorable safety profile and suppression of T cell-dependent antibody responses in healthy adults.

Tonix Pharmaceuticals plans to initiate a Phase 2 investigator-initiated study for TNX-1500 in adult kidney transplant rejection at Massachusetts General Hospital in the second half of 2026 pending FDA clearance.

Zenas BioPharma submitted a Biologics License Application (BLA) to the U.S. FDA for obexelimab, a B-cell inhibitor monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of Immunoglobulin G4-Related Disease (IgG4-RD).

Tiziana Life Sciences completed enrollment in its Phase 2a INFORM-MS trial for intranasal foralumab, a fully human anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody, in patients with non-active Secondary Progressive Multiple Sclerosis (na-SPMS).
Zenas BioPharma's Phase 3 INDIGO trial for obexelimab met its primary endpoint with a 56% reduction in IgG4-RD flare risk versus placebo (HR 0.443; p=0.0005).
Topline data for Tiziana Life Sciences' intranasal foralumab Phase 2a trial is expected in late Q3 2026, with results scheduled for presentation at the ACTRIMS/ECTRIMS meeting in October 2026.

Therini Bio dosed first patients in Phase 1b trial of THN391, a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting fibrin's inflammatory epitope for Diabetic Macular Edema (DME), with initial data expected Q4 2026.
Therini Bio advanced THN622, a fibrin/VEGF bispecific antibody, as lead candidate for retinal neurodegeneration, combining VEGF blockade with fibrin inhibition.

A 2024 review in Graefe's Archive for Clinical and Experimental Ophthalmology identified alemtuzumab and immune checkpoint inhibitors as monoclonal antibodies associated with medication-induced thyroid eye disease (TED).
Alemtuzumab treatment resulted in autoimmune thyroid disorder in up to 33% of patients, with Graves' disease accounting for 63% of cases, according to a systematic review of 23 studies.

University of Pennsylvania researchers identified GPNMB as a key protein driving Parkinson's disease spread, with early experiments showing monoclonal antibodies blocking the protein halted toxic cell-to-cell transmission.

Mitchell E. Horwitz (Duke University) discussed ongoing pilot studies evaluating the addition of a co-stimulatory blocking monoclonal antibody to reduce graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) risk in cord blood-derived transplantation.

Tonix Pharmaceuticals — TNX-1500 — Kidney transplant rejection / Autoimmune diseases — Phase 1 data published in Journal of Clinical Immunology; third-generation Fc-modified anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody showed favorable safety, suppressed TDARs, and supports monthly IV dosing; Phase 2 study expected H2 2026.
Zenas BioPharma — Obexelimab — IgG4-Related Disease — BLA submitted to FDA; Phase 3 INDIGO trial met primary endpoint with 56% flare risk reduction (HR 0.443; p=0.0005) vs placebo.
Tiziana Life Sciences — Foralumab (intranasal) — Non-active Secondary Progressive Multiple Sclerosis — Phase 2a INFORM-MS trial fully enrolled (48 patients); topline data expected late Q3 2026.

Therini Bio — THN391 — Diabetic Macular Edema — Phase 1b trial initiated; humanized monoclonal antibody blocking fibrin inflammatory epitope; three monthly intravitreal injections; initial data Q4 2026.
Therini Bio — THN622 — Retinal Neurodegeneration — Lead candidate selection completed; fibrin/VEGF bispecific antibody; targets vascular dysfunction in DME.
Alemtuzumab — Thyroid Eye Disease — Adverse event profile — Patients developed thyroid dysfunction 26.3 months post-initiation, progressing to orbitopathy after an additional 19.9 months on average.

University of Pennsylvania — GPNMB-targeting monoclonal antibodies — Parkinson's disease — Preclinical study demonstrated that blocking GPNMB with antibodies stopped the spread of alpha-synuclein pathology between neurons in lab models.

Duke University / Mitchell E. Horwitz — Co-stimulatory blocking monoclonal antibody — Graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) prophylaxis in cord blood transplantation — Pilot study ongoing to assess feasibility of adding agent to limit GVHD risk.

Tonix Pharmaceuticals — Development of third-generation anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody — TNX-1500 — Phase 1 data supports safety and pharmacokinetics; Phase 2 kidney transplant study pending FDA IND clearance at MGH.
Zenas BioPharma — Regulatory submission for autoimmune therapy — Obexelimab (anti-CD19/CD20 bispecific mAb) — BLA filed for IgG4-RD following positive Phase 3 INDIGO results.
Tiziana Life Sciences — Clinical advancement of neurodegenerative candidate — Foralumab (fully human anti-CD3 mAb) — Phase 2a enrollment completed for na-SPMS; open-label extension phase initiated.

Therini Bio — Fibrin-targeting immunotherapy development — THN391 (monoclonal antibody) + THN622 (bispecific antibody) — Phase 1b dosing initiated for DME; preclinical data showed efficacy comparable to VEGF antagonists.

Tonix Pharmaceuticals — Development of third-generation anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody — TNX-1500 — Phase 1 results support progression to Phase 2 for organ transplant rejection prevention; no approved anti-CD40L mAbs currently exist.
Zenas BioPharma — Commercialization of first-in-class bispecific monoclonal antibody — Obexelimab (anti-CD19/CD32b) — BLA filed for IgG4-RD; Phase 3 INDIGO data to be presented at EULAR 2026.

TNX-1500 — Mechanism: Fc-modified IgG4 anti-CD40L (CD154) monoclonal antibody — Phase: 1 (completed) — Population: Healthy adults — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints/Results: Generally well tolerated, suppressed primary and secondary T cell-dependent antibody responses (TDARs) to KLH, demonstrated half-life supporting monthly intravenous dosing.

Obexelimab — Mechanism: B-cell inhibition (anti-CD19/CD20) — Phase: 3 — Population: IgG4-Related Disease — Comparator: Placebo — Results: 56% reduction in flare risk (HR 0.443; p=0.0005); Grade ≥3 TEAEs 11.3% vs 23.7%.

Foralumab — Mechanism: Fully human anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody — Phase: 2a — Population: Non-active Secondary Progressive Multiple Sclerosis — Comparator: Placebo — Design: 48 patients, 12-week treatment, PET/MRI assessments; topline data expected late Q3 2026.

THN391 — Mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody blocking fibrin inflammatory epitope without disrupting coagulation — Phase: 1b — Population: Diabetic Macular Edema — Comparator: VEGF antagonists (preclinical) — Results: As effective as VEGF antagonists in containing neovascular leakage in preclinical models.

THN622 — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting fibrin inflammatory epitope and VEGF — Phase: Preclinical/Lead Candidate — Population: Retinal conditions with vascular dysfunction (DME) — Comparator: Standard of care (VEGF antagonists) — Endpoint: Dual mechanism combining VEGF blockade with fibrin inhibition.

Alemtuzumab — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody (anti-CD52) — Indication: Multiple sclerosis — Adverse Event: Thyroid Eye Disease (TED) — Incidence: Up to 33% of patients developed autoimmune thyroid disorder, primarily Graves' disease.
Ipilimumab, tremelimumab, durvalumab, nivolumab — Mechanism: Immune checkpoint inhibitor monoclonal antibodies — Adverse Event: Induce 'TED-like' orbitopathy without underlying thyroid dysfunction.

GPNMB-targeting antibodies — Mechanism: Block glycoprotein nonmetastatic melanoma B to prevent alpha-synuclein spread — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Neuronal cell models of Parkinson's disease — Comparator: Unblocked controls — Results: Antibody blockade halted toxic process and cell-to-cell transmission in early experiments.

Co-stimulatory blocking monoclonal antibody — Mechanism: Co-stimulation blockade to limit immune activation — Phase: Pilot study — Population: Cord blood transplantation recipients — Goal: Reduction of graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) risk.
